# PyClickHouseMigrator

> Lightweight, ORM-free Python CLI for ClickHouse schema migrations.
> SQL-first workflow with checksum validation, baseline, advisory locking, and cluster support.

- Python 3.11+
- Dependencies: `clickhouse-driver`, `click`
- CLI entry point: `migrator`
- PyPI package: `py-clickhouse-migrator`
- Version: 1.1.0

## Core Model

- Only `.sql` migration files are discovered
- Canonical filename: `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_name.sql`
- Each file must contain exactly one `-- migrator:up` and one `-- migrator:down`
- Every non-empty SQL statement block must start with `-- @stmt`
- Empty `down` is allowed
- Pending migrations are applied in `sorted(filename)` order

## Commands

- `init`
- `new`
- `up`
- `rollback`
- `show`
- `baseline`
- `repair`
- `force-unlock`
- `lock-info`

## Important Behavior

- `baseline` writes ledger rows for an existing database without executing migration SQL
- `show` marks `HEAD`, `baseline`, `modified`, and `missing`
- Checksums are computed from parsed statement lists, not raw file bytes
- Cluster mode creates replicated service tables with `ON CLUSTER`
- Legacy `.py` migrations are outside the supported workflow and are not discovered by the current runner

## Local Dev

- `uv sync --dev`
- `pre-commit install`
- `make lint`
- `make test`
- `make test-cluster`

## Docs

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Maksim-Burtsev/PyClickHouseMigrator/master/README.md)
